dialogues: You saw this bar graph in Warmup. Now let's learn how to read it exactly.
See these numbers along the side? They show the scale. 10, 20, 30, 40. That's counting by 10s!
Red has 40. The height of the bar ends at the 40 line.
I can count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40. You already know how to count by 10s!
Look at Blue. The bar's height ends at a line on the axis. What value does this show?
Right. 30. The bar ends at the 30 line. Same as 3 symbols on the picture graph—each symbol shows 10.
Green shows 50. Let's count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50.
You already know how to count by 10s. That's exactly what we do with scale of 10. The fourth bar shows Yellow. Count by 10s. What value does the bar show?
Right. 20. Two jumps of 10: 10, 20.
Now you create a bar. Swings has 30 votes. Set the HEIGHT for Swings to show 30.
Right. 10, 20, 30. The bar ends at 30 on the axis. 30 votes for Swings.
Nonfiction has 60 books. That's the biggest number. Set the bar for Nonfiction.
Right. Nonfiction at 60. You found the 60 line and set the bar there.
Here's the completed graph. Use it to answer a question. How many MORE books were Nonfiction than Comics?
Right. Nonfiction is 60, Comics is 20. The difference is 40.
You can read bars that end right at the lines. But what if a bar ends between the lines?
Look at Jake's bar. It doesn't end at a line. It ends between the lines.
Remember half-symbols from scale of 2? Half of 2 was 1. Same idea here. Half of 10 is 5.
Half of 10 is 5. So between 20 and 30, that's 20 + 5 = 25. Jake has 25 stickers.
Now look at Leo's bar. It's also between the lines. Leo's bar is between 30 and 40. What value does this show?
Right. 35. It's halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 is 35.
Team A scored 45 points. Set the bar for Team A at 45, halfway between 40 and 50.
That's it. 45, right between the lines, halfway from 40 to 50.
Runner C completed 35 laps. Set the bar for Runner C to show 35.
Right. 35. Between 30 and 40, right in the middle.
Some values are at the axis lines. Some are between. You decide where each bar goes. Try to complete the graph.
Right. Monday and Wednesday are at the axis lines. Tuesday and Thursday are between the lines. You figured out which is which.
Use this graph to answer. How many TOTAL minutes of reading for Monday and Tuesday?
That's right. 75 minutes total. Monday is 40, Tuesday is 35. 40 plus 35 is 75.
You've made vertical bar graphs. Now let's try one that goes sideways. HORIZONTAL.
This bar graph is horizontal. The bars go sideways, but you read them the same way. Find where the bar ends on the scale.
Zoo's bar extends to 45. Between 40 and 50, halfway. What value does the Museum bar show?
Right. 30. The bar ends right at the 30 line.
Look at the Park bar. What value does the Park bar represent?
Right. 55. Halfway between 50 and 60.
Create the whole graph. Some values are at axis lines, some are between.
Nice. Ava at 50, right at the line. Kai, Noah, and Zoe all between the lines.
Use the graph you made. How many MORE miles did Ava walk than Noah?
Right. 25 more miles. Ava walked 50, Noah walked 25. The difference is 25.
Use the data table to complete the graph.
Right. All four bars match the data. You built the graph from the table.
How many plants in all for Peppers and Carrots?
That's it. 80 plants in all. 35 plus 45 is 80.
You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You used the axis to find values, even values between the lines. Let's see what you know.
